name: Billy Cobham
abstract: William Emanuel Cobham Jr. (born May 16, 1944) is a Panamanian–American jazz drummer who came to prominence in the late 1960s and early 1970s with trumpeter Miles Davis and then with the Mahavishnu Orchestra.

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Junior Mance Trio

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: Then I did the one with Billy Cobham, where it’s titled, When Cobham Meets Bellson. We did those in Europe.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: He was around twenty years — but you know it was a fantastic band — Billy Cobham fresh out of the Army on drums... So I couldn’t have asked for more
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: So, we replaced Bar Phillips with Ron Carter, and after Freddie Waits left, replaced him with Billy Cobham. So, we had a very good group.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: You have Herbie Hancock on piano, Ron Carter, bass, Billy Cobham, drums. So, George Benson leading... That’s right.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: And before that I had worked with Billy Cobham, which was a great experience.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: But I think Ray said it, he said “well if you drew the plans in ‘38 then you’re the first one.” Because what I did, I just did a thing with just the two bass drums, but you came out with a whole set, the whole thing, see? So the players that followed me on that were Eddie Shaughnessy and the Sam Woo
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996
